resolution_notes: Section 6.8 has already been rewritten to address all issues from the curmudgeon c2 review. Verified against current part6 HTML: (1) WIN attribution corrected from WIN-012 to WIN-053 — title now reads "The WIN-053 Single-Pole Collapse". (2) Mathematical error fixed — no longer claims single exponential; correctly states "a sum of a decaying and a growing exponential, producing a U-shaped (cosh-like) field profile" with computed values (~68,700 nT north, ~39,800 nT equator, ~71,000 nT rim). (3) IGRF coefficient count corrected to 195. (4) Flux conservation argument added as the lead physics critique (1,660:1 self-contradiction). (5) Toroidal 1/r vs exponential incompatibility added with Griffiths reference. (6) Quantitative IGRF comparison added (195 coefficients, ~50 nT RMS globally vs dome 20% RMS ~10,000-13,000 nT). All curmudgeon-recommended stronger arguments have been incorporated.
holes_addressed: Major: Single-exponential mathematical error replaced with correct cosh-like analysis
Minor: r_S parameterization clarified (r_S = R − r_N, R = 20,015 km)
Stronger argument added: flux conservation 1,660:1 self-contradiction
Stronger argument added: toroidal 1/r vs exponential incompatibility
Stronger argument added: quantitative IGRF comparison (200x worse RMS)
